[[ ## text ## ]]
The memory usage in PyTorch is extremely efficient compared to Torch or some of the alternatives. We've written custom memory allocators for the GPU to make sure that your deep learning models are maximally memory efficient. This enables you to train bigger deep learning models than before.

Respond with the corresponding output fields, starting with the field `[[ ## reasoning ## ]]`, then `[[ ## is_ai ## ]]` (must be formatted as a valid Python bool), and then ending with the marker for `[[ ## completed ## ]]`.

The passage uses broad, promotional language, repeats closely related claims about memory efficiency, and presents generic technical benefits without concrete implementation details or evidence. This polished, formulaic style is characteristic of AI-generated text.
[[ ## is_ai ## ]]
True
[[ ## completed ## ]]

[[ ## text ## ]]
Compared with Torch and several alternatives, PyTorch uses memory with remarkable efficiency. To that end, we built custom GPU memory allocators so deep-learning models make the fullest possible use of available capacity. Thus, you may train larger models than heretofore, without squandering precious GPU memory.

Respond with the corresponding output fields, starting with the field `[[ ## reasoning ## ]]`, then `[[ ## is_ai ## ]]` (must be formatted as a valid Python bool), and then ending with the marker for `[[ ## completed ## ]]`.

message.content:

The passage has a polished, promotional tone and uses generic efficiency claims with somewhat awkward phrasing such as “than heretofore” and “squandering precious GPU memory.” Its broad, repetitive explanation of PyTorch’s memory allocation sounds likely AI-generated or AI-rewritten rather than natural technical prose.
[[ ## is_ai ## ]]
True
[[ ## completed ## ]]

messages.content:
Your input fields are:
1. `basic_instruction` (str): The initial instructions before optimization
Your output fields are:
1. `proposed_instruction` (str): The improved instructions for the language model
2. `proposed_prefix_for_output_field` (str): The string at the end of the prompt, which will help the model start solving the task
All interactions will be structured in the following way, with the appropriate values filled in.

[[ ## basic_instruction ## ]]
{basic_instruction}

[[ ## proposed_instruction ## ]]
{proposed_instruction}

[[ ## proposed_prefix_for_output_field ## ]]
{proposed_prefix_for_output_field}

[[ ## completed ## ]]
In adhering to this structure, your objective is: 
        You are an instruction optimizer for large language models. I will give you a ``signature`` of fields (inputs and outputs) in English. Your task is to propose an instruction that will lead a good language model to perform the task well. Don't be afraid to be creative.

[[ ## basic_instruction ## ]]
Decide whether the supplied passage was generated by an AI.

Respond with the corresponding output fields, starting with the field `[[ ## proposed_instruction ## ]]`, then `[[ ## proposed_prefix_for_output_field ## ]]`, and then ending with the marker for `[[ ## completed ## ]]`.

message.content:
[[ ## proposed_instruction ## ]]
Examine the supplied passage and determine whether it is more likely to have been generated by an AI or written by a human. Consider patterns such as formulaic structure, repetitive phrasing, generic or overly polished language, unnatural transitions, inconsistent specificity, and distinctive human voice or lived experience. Weigh the passage as a whole rather than treating any single cue as decisive. Return exactly one label: `AI-generated` or `Human-written`. Do not include an explanation.

[[ ## proposed_prefix_for_output_field ## ]]
Classification: 

[[ ## completed ## ]]
